conditions.critical_condition_basis: elution volumes independent of molar mass
conditions.detector: Waters 486 tunable UV detector at 235 nm
conditions.evidence_text: For the analysis of the PLA’s LC-CC conditions have been used corresponding to silica gel as the stationary phase and a mixture of 1,4-dioxane/n-hexane (56.25/43.75 by vol%) at 50 8C as the mobile phase. At the critical point of adsorption, a series of linear C4H9-PLA-OH’s having molar masses (Mn) in the range from 2.3 £ 10[3] to 7.4 £ 10[4] , prepared by ringopening polymerization of LA initiated with Sn(OC4H9)2 (THF, 80 8C), showed no dependence of the elution volumes on molar mass.
conditions.physical_address: Department of Polymer Chemistry, Center of Molecular and Macromolecular Studies, Polish Academy of Sciences, Sienkiewicza 112, PL-90-363 Lodz, Poland

conditions.critical_condition_basis: elution volumes increase with number of PLA-OH arms
conditions.detector: Waters 486 tunable UV detector at 235 nm
conditions.evidence_text: The chromatograph for LC-CC measurements consisted of a Waters model 510 pump, a Rheodyne injection valve with a 100 ml loop, two columns: Si-100 and Si-300 (Macherey-Nagel), and Waters 486 tunable UV detector operating at 235 nm. A mixture of 1,4-dioxane and n- hexane (56.25:43.75 vol%) was used as mobile phase with flow rate of 0.5 ml/min. The temperature of the columns was set at 50 8C using a Waters column oven.
conditions.physical_address: Department of Polymer Chemistry, Center of Molecular and Macromolecular Studies, Polish Academy of Sciences, Sienkiewicza 112, PL-90-363 Lodz, Poland
